data.title: Bitcoin Valuation Report for Q2 2026: $143,000, with potential for more than double the upside.
data.description:
Authors: Daniel Kim, Ryan Yoon, Jay Jo; Source: Tiger Research; Compiled by: Shaw & Jinse Finance

Key Summary

  • Despite slowing growth, the macroeconomy remains supportive: Global M2 money supply hit a record high of $134.4 trillion, and ETF inflows turned net positive for the first time in 14 months. However, the plunge in Iranian oil prices led to a rise in the March CPI to 3.3%, narrowing the Fed's room for interest rate cuts.

  • Bitcoin on-chain indicators are shifting from undervaluation to early equilibrium: Key on-chain indicators have moved out of the panic zone of the first quarter.

  • Bitcoin on-chain indicators are shifting from undervaluation to early equilibrium The current price is $70,500, about 13% lower than the average entry price of $78,000 for long-term holders. A break above this level would be a major signal of a recent trend reversal. The $143,000 target price and 2x upside remain valid: This target price is calculated based on a neutral benchmark price of $132,500, with fundamental factors down by 10% and macro factors up by 20%. While down from the Q1 target price of $185,500, the larger spot pullback means that the upside potential has actually expanded from the current level. Macroeconomic positives remain, but the pace is slowing. Since the Q1 report was released, the price of Bitcoin has fallen by about 27%, with the average trading price in early April approaching $70,500. The Iranian conflict has introduced a new variable, but the overall macroeconomic environment remains favorable. What has changed is not the direction, but the pace. Liquidity hits record high, yet fails to flow to Bitcoin. As of February 2026, global M2 money supply continued to grow, approaching a record high of $134 trillion. However, the price of Bitcoin fell by 27% compared to the first quarter. Liquidity and price movements are diametrically opposed. The source of liquidity explains this discrepancy. Over the past year, more than 60% of the M2 growth in the four largest economies (China, the United States, the Eurozone, and Japan) came from China, thanks to the People's Bank of China's reduction of the reserve requirement ratio and formal easing policies in the first quarter. The United States contributed only 10%. The problem is that the channels for liquidity from within China to flow into the Bitcoin market are limited, cryptocurrency trading restrictions remain in place, and indirect channels via Hong Kong and Singapore mainly serve institutional fund flows. Global liquidity is at historical highs, but the proportion of funds actually flowing into Bitcoin has declined. The Iranian conflict slows the Fed's rate cut pace. With liquidity from China largely blocked, dollar liquidity remains the main driver of Bitcoin. Even so, the Iranian conflict has slowed this process. Following the US-Israel airstrikes against Iran on February 28, the Strait of Hormuz was blocked. In mid-March, Brent crude oil prices surged to $118 per barrel, while Dubai crude oil prices hit a record high of $166 per barrel. This shock directly pushed up inflation. The U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) jumped from 2.4% in February to 3.3% in March, a two-year high. The Federal Reserve's room for interest rate cuts also narrowed. The March dot plot showed that the market expected the Fed to cut rates only once in 2026. Despite this, the direction of the accommodative policy remained unchanged. In mid-April, the Strait of Hormuz partially reopened, and oil prices fell sharply to around $90 per barrel. Core CPI remained stable at 2.6%, meaning the shock had not yet spread to broader economic sectors. President Trump formally nominated Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chairman in late January, and the Senate confirmation hearings are still ongoing. Powell's term ends on May 15, and the accommodative policy bias is likely to continue. The number of rate cuts may decrease, but the direction remains the same. Institutional Fund Flows Reverse [Image of an image: https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454577_watermarknone.png](https://img.jinse.com.cn/https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454577_watermarknone.png) [Image of an image: t9dVrhf4UOH9zi2tCVf3jspZgBLJpg2f6rQmXZzA.png](https://img.jinse.com.cn/7454577_watermarknone.png)] The institutional fund outflows that led to the first quarter's decline have begun to reverse. Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced their worst monthly outflows since their launch in November 2025, and had maintained net outflows for five consecutive months. However, since March, monthly net inflows have turned positive. By mid-April, year-to-date cumulative inflows had also turned positive, with total assets under management rebounding to $96.5 billion. Corporate Bitcoin holdings are also accelerating. Strategy spent $2.54 billion to purchase 34,164 Bitcoins in just one week (April 13-19), bringing its total holdings to 815,061. However, it's worth noting that the number of companies participating in this trend did not increase significantly. Macroeconomic Indicators Downgraded to +20% Structural positive factors remain: liquidity expansion, a policy easing bias, a recovery in institutional inflows, and progress on the US CLARITY Act. Short-term negative factors such as the Iranian-induced oil crisis and the slowdown in the Fed's interest rate cuts partially offset these positives. The adjusted macroeconomic indicators for the second quarter were revised down by 5% to +20% from the first quarter. From Undervaluation to Early Equilibrium On-chain indicators have moved away from extreme panic and are transitioning towards the boundary between undervaluation and equilibrium. Key indicators, including MVRV-Z, NUPL, and aSOPR, have broken through the panic zone of the first quarter and entered the early recovery phase. A sharp rally similar to that seen in panic-driven rebounds is unlikely, but the region's historical one-year average return has consistently remained in double digits. Currently, the risk-reward ratio remains very favorable. It's worth noting that the average cost base of short-term holders (STH) has been gradually declining. This indicates that speculative capital is exiting, while new buyers are entering at lower prices. This timing coincides with the recovery in ETF net inflows and large-scale buying by strategies, confirming the view that institutional buyers have been buying at a discount and lowering the average purchase price. The key risk level is $54,000, the average cost base. A break below this threshold would result in unrealized losses across the entire network, thus constituting a bottom in extreme scenarios. The strongest resistance level is at $78,000, coinciding with the average purchase price of long-term holders. The current price is $70,500, about 13% below this level, and a significant amount of recently injected short-term capital is currently in an unrealized loss position. A decisive break above $78,000 in the short term warrants close attention. Superficial Growth, Substantial Stagnation Bitcoin's average daily trading volume reached 564,000 transactions in the first half of April, a year-on-year increase of 37.9%. While the data appears strong on the surface, the details tell a different story. The number of active addresses decreased to 428,000 during the same period, a year-on-year decrease of 13.2% and a month-on-month decrease of 4.2%. The average transaction value dropped to 1.19 BTC, a 34.1% decrease from 1.80 BTC in the previous quarter. While the number of transactions increased, both the number of participants and the average transaction value decreased. This trend reflects a small number of users repeatedly transferring small amounts of funds, rather than the broader economic use of the network. The increase in transaction volume is likely primarily attributable to mechanical fund flows such as exchange deposits, and is unrelated to actual growth. The Q1 report maintained fundamental indicators at 0%, based on expectations of expansion in the BTCFi ecosystem. However, this expectation weakened in Q2. According to The Block's "2026 Digital Asset Outlook," Bitcoin's total value locked (TVL) at Level 2 has decreased by 74% year-to-date, and BTCFi's total TVL has decreased by 10%, representing only 0.46% of Bitcoin's total supply (91,332 coins). While individual protocols like Babylon and Lombard have shown selective growth, the entire ecosystem has shrunk. Fundamental Indicators Downgraded to -10% Superficial growth has not translated into actual network expansion, and the data supporting the BTCFi theory has weakened. The balance between positive and negative signals supporting the 0% adjustment in Q1 has been broken. Therefore, the fundamental indicators for Q2 have been downgraded from 0% to -10%. Target Price $143,000, 2x Upside Potential Applying the time value method to the average price in early April 2026 yields a neutral benchmark price of $132,500. With fundamental indicators adjusted by -10% and macro indicators adjusted by +20%, the 12-month target price is set at $143,000. This figure is approximately 23% lower than the Q1 target price of $185,500. However, the actual upside potential has expanded. On average, the upside has increased from 93% in Q1 to 103% in Q2. The lower target price does not reflect pessimism. Macroeconomic direction and on-chain structure continue to support a bullish medium- to long-term view. Three key near-term milestones: 1) A breakout above the network's medium-term equilibrium price of $78,000; 2) Continued ETF inflows; 3) A shift in Fed policy following reduced geopolitical risks. If all three conditions are met, the $143,000 target price remains achievable.

data.title: The remarkable phenomenon of Chinese AI going global: priced three times higher than domestically, yet still snapped up by foreign programmers.
data.description:
Author: Zhou Ailin, Tencent News "Deep Dive"

When ChatGPT first emerged, Chinese users went to great lengths to find overseas Apple IDs and phone numbers to try it out. Now, the tables have turned. With the arrival of the AI ​​Agent era, token cost-effectiveness has become key, and Chinese large-scale models have attracted the attention of overseas programmers.

Several IT engineers and AI investors mentioned to Tencent News "Deep Dive" that for general programming tasks, domestic large-scale models are indeed capable of handling them. According to Tencent News "Deep Dive's" observations, Zhipu AI has attracted global programmers with its vertical advantages in the programming field. Due to the significant price increase of its overseas version of GLM Coding Plan, which is nearly three times the price of the domestic version, many foreigners have begun to study how to secure quotas for the Chinese version.

“Why is Zhipu GLM Coding Plan now priced similarly to Claude Code?” an overseas netizen asked on X.

In less than six months, the overseas version of GLM rose from $49/month to $160/month, an increase of 226%; while Claude remained stable at $200/month. "I managed to snag a super discounted GLM version last year for only $360 a year," another user replied. Now, the highest-end version of the Max Coding Plan is priced close to $1600 per year. "You could try the Chinese version; it has the same content, but the price is only around 4500 RMB per year," another foreign user suggested. Such conversations are common on overseas social media platforms. The high demand for the Zhipu Coding Plan has led to a shortage of computing power, requiring users to be on call at 10 AM every day to secure a spot, even giving rise to scalper services. This has dispelled earlier market concerns that Chinese AI products lacked consumer demand. Conversely, while API pricing increased by 83%, the daily average number of paid tokens still quadrupled. For this reason, Zhipu's target price has been raised several times. JPMorgan Chase recently gave a target price of HK$950, which is more than double the price at the beginning of the year.

GLM domestic version

Price Comparison between Domestic and Overseas Versions

Chinese AI Models Gain Popularity Overseas

Grab a quota, learn verification, and overcome payment challenges

However, to obtain a high-value model package, overseas users still need to overcome several hurdles.

The first is grabbing a quota. Zhipu uses a "hunger marketing" strategy of releasing quotas daily at 10 AM for new domestic users, mainly targeting new subscribers and some users upgrading their packages.

Why the limited supply?

Industry insiders told Tencent News's "Deep Dive" that there are three main real reasons: Firstly, the cost of computing power is outweighed by the cost, and limiting usage is a "stopgap" measure. GLM-5.1 is comparable to Claude Opus, with extremely high costs per call. The domestic Max plan costs 469 yuan/month, but the theoretical usable quota is equivalent to 15-30 times the monthly fee—meaning that for 469 yuan per month, users can actually consume approximately 7,000-14,000 yuan worth of computing resources. Secondly, it may be a deliberate attempt to create a sense of scarcity to accelerate the development of "pay-to-use habits." Thirdly, there is a genuine surge in demand, with supply unable to keep up. Moreover, during peak hours (2:00 PM - 6:00 PM), GLM-5.1 consumes three times the resources. At the 2025 earnings conference, Zhipu CEO Zhang Peng disclosed, "API call pricing increased by 83% in the first quarter. Even so, call volume increased by 400%, and demand still exceeds supply." Currently, the overseas version does not require timed purchases, but the pricing is much higher, likely due to break-even considerations. Besides securing quotas, registering on the Chinese website with an overseas mobile number is not difficult, as Zhipu still accepts mobile numbers from all over the world. However, authentication is the biggest headache for foreigners. For example, the Zhipu platform requires real-name authentication before payment, and the authentication process is mainly in Chinese, requiring foreigners to use translation tools (Chrome Translate/DeepL) for assistance. Foreigners' identity verification only supports passport authentication, not direct binding with an overseas credit card to bypass real-name authentication. Many foreigners still feel uncomfortable providing passport information. The "hurdles" don't end there; payment is the biggest barrier. The website accepts WeChat Pay, Alipay, and UnionPay, but does not support international payment methods most familiar to foreigners, such as Visa/Mastercard direct card payments, PayPal, and Stripe. So, can foreigners use Alipay/WeChat Pay? Since 2024, China has simplified the threshold for foreigners to use Alipay/WeChat, but restrictions remain. According to feedback, Alipay is relatively user-friendly for foreigners; uploading a passport is sufficient for verification, with a single transaction limit of $5,000 and an annual limit of $50,000 (enough to cover subscription fees). In contrast, WeChat Pay's single transaction limit is only 6,000 yuan (approximately $830), which is sufficient but tight for the Max plan's annual payment, with an annual limit of only 60,000 yuan (approximately $8,300). After subscribing for several months, the limit may be reached, and it requires additional activation of the "International Transaction" switch, a step many foreigners are unaware of.

Comparison of different payment methods. Source: Compiled by Tencent News "Deep Dive"

Another issue is that the bigmodel.cn console page is entirely in Chinese, which may pose a challenge for foreigners. Viewing usage and managing subscriptions requires translation assistance. However, compared to the previous challenges, this is relatively easier to overcome.

Another issue is that the bigmodel.cn console page is entirely in Chinese, which may pose a challenge for foreigners. Viewing usage and managing subscriptions requires translation assistance.

Revenue forecast adjustment. Source: JPMorgan Chase, compiled by Tencent News "Deep Dive"

HSBC's latest report also gave a target price of HK$920, and stated that the rapid growth of Anthropic's programming-related revenue validates the huge global demand for "Vibe Coding" and intelligent agent engineering. Zhipu estimates that the global enterprise AI programming market will reach $200 billion by 2030.

From its launch in September 2025 to March 2026, the GLM Coding Plan saw a 15-fold increase in token usage and over 240,000 paying users, with price increases not impacting user volume, validating the model's capabilities. GLM-5.1 launched on March 27, 2026, achieving a programming test score of 45.3 (Claude Opus 4.6 scored 47.9, a very small difference). Initially, investors clearly favored MiniMax due to its multimodal advantages and nearly 70% of its revenue coming from overseas. However, HSBC now states it prefers Zhipu over MiniMax, citing: Zhipu's valuation is more reasonable; Zhipu's record of price increases not impacting user volume is more convincing; MiniMax's growth relies heavily on video generation and companionship applications, facing IP infringement risks and fierce competition from ByteDance/Kuaishou; and MiniMax has a higher lock-up period, putting greater pressure on its share price. However, listed model companies in China still face challenges. For example, Zhipu stated that current actual demand exceeds computing power supply by 1-2 times, and they are addressing the supply-demand imbalance by prioritizing core customers and raising prices. The computing power bottleneck continues to constrain revenue. Furthermore, competition is intensifying, with rivals (Anthropic, OpenAI, Dark Side of the Moon, and Leap Star) about to go public, potentially weakening Zhipu's scarcity premium. The lifting of share lock-up periods for two batches of major shareholders on July 7, 2026, and January 7, 2027, may put selling pressure on Zhipu. More importantly, the current model iteration is rapid; if subsequent performance falls short of expectations, coupled with high cash consumption, refinancing needs, and geopolitical risks, all of these could put downward pressure on revenue and stock price. Wang Lisheng, a partner at TSVC, a well-known Silicon Valley venture capital firm, told Tencent News's "Deep Dive" that while Chinese models are constantly being optimized, their stability may still need improvement. In high-value work, the cost may not necessarily be low, and the memory mechanism for long texts is also a key issue. A value system still needs to be established in the future.

data.title: Predicting Market Misconceptions: It's Not Over-Financialization, It's Subjectivity and Truth Discovery
data.description:
Source: Jeff Park, Bitwise Consultant; Compiled by: Jinse Finance Claw

Last week, both Axios and More Perfect Union (MPU) stepped forward to explain to the public what prediction markets are. While Axios' Dan Primack attempted to provide a neutral platform for the debate with the founder of Kalshi (although his bias was quite transparent), MPU's Trevor Hayes took a more direct stance, portraying prediction markets as a "social cancer."

Frankly, I have some sympathy for both perspectives. As someone whose career has been at the intersection of Wall Street and crypto, I understand the growing societal concern about "over-financialization," a trend that is fueling a culture of "gambling-induced public health crisis."

Frankly, I have some sympathy for both viewpoints. As someone whose career is at the intersection of Wall Street and crypto, I understand the growing societal concern about "over-financialization," a trend that is fueling a culture of "gambling-induced public health crisis."

However, these journalists often make the mistake of presupposing conclusions and then looking for "accomplices," frequently conflating multiple issues within an oversimplified narrative. One moment we're discussing "insider trading," the next it's "online casinos," and finally it boils down to "gambling addiction." But this is precisely the misunderstanding most people have about prediction markets: regardless of how you view the downsides of over-financialization (through ODTE options, swap-based ETFs, meme stocks, etc.), the story of prediction markets should be celebrated as a driver of **high agency**, **truth discovery**, and **decentralized moral rights**. My article below attempts to dissect this viewpoint more deeply. The Blurred Line Between “Investing” and “Gambling” Defining whether something is “investing” or “gambling” depends entirely on whether you believe the behavior has a “positive expected value” (+EV), not on whether the system itself is deterministic or random. In other words, it is defined by the player, not by the game. Let’s take a closer look. The first thing I noticed in my coverage of MPU was that Trevor Hayes often began his questions with “Since predicting the market is obviously gambling…” as if it were an established fact. This fundamental assumption needs to be examined first. The biggest trend in the financial world over the past two decades has been that the clear line between “investing” and “gambling” has become increasingly blurred. Consider the following facts: 1) 60% of US stock trading volume is high-frequency trading (HFT), monopolized by institutions like Jane Street and Citadel in an oligopolistic structure; 2) Passive ETFs account for over 90% of ETF assets under management (although active strategies are beginning a belated rebound); 3) The average holding period for US stocks has shortened from nine years in the mid-1970s to just about six months in 2025! Meanwhile, driven by algorithmic trading, daily trading volume has more than tripled in the past decade. On top of these figures, there's an unstoppable trend: retail investor trading activity will exceed $5 trillion in 2025, an increase of about 50% from 2023. However, you won't find many experts jumping out to accuse "stock trading" of gambling. Why? Because most people agree that stock picking is not gambling, because (presumably) it requires skill. Here's a key understanding: the term "gambling" becomes unfair in its description because it conflates "skillful games" with "purely probabilistic games." For example, slot machines and poker are both called gambling, but many people intuitively see the unfairness—slot machines are based on pure luck with a negative expected value (-EV) strategy, while poker can be based on actual skill with a positive expected value (+EV) strategy. To put it bluntly, defining "investment" and "gambling" depends primarily on whether one believes the strategy allows for a positive expected value. It has nothing to do with the "game" itself, whether it's deterministic (pure risk value arbitrage and slot machines are) or random (stock picking and poker are). Predicting markets, like poker, is a type of random game with deterministic components. Whether you consider it "gambling" or "investing" depends entirely on the player—that is, you. It depends on whether you are a highly subjective, highly skilled individual or a less subjective, less skilled individual. This leads to the second question: if we consider gambling to be player-driven "speculation," then how exactly does such a market operate? And who provides the liquidity? "The other side of speculation is insurance." All financial innovations initially resemble gambling. The early stock market was, as was the futures market (rife with rampant insider trading), and of course, the modern commodities market (where classic insider trading is almost impossible to define). This is precisely because the other side of speculation is insurance. They are two sides of the same coin, as it is a zero-sum game defined by a strict synthetic risk transfer. Moreover, not all "information" naturally originates from private enterprises. This leads to the next question often raised by critics of prediction markets: "Some markets are purely speculative in function and should not exist because they create no value for society." Sports betting is most frequently targeted. Because sports are entertainment, betting on entertainment is considered fundamentally unproductive. However, this view is incorrect. Entertainment is social consumption. Some would even argue that entertainment is a fundamental reason why humans find life fulfilling. More importantly, entertainment itself is economic consumption, meaning it has a two-sided market. The sports industry generates over $50 billion in revenue, and if you include the surrounding ecosystem (media, equipment, apparel, nutritional supplements, etc.), the figure is estimated to be over $1 trillion. Take Nike, for example; it pays millions of dollars in sponsorship fees to players and teams, and they have a real economic interest in how they allocate capital (and how they hedge risk), all based on the outcomes of sporting events and their players. Today, society is widely brainwashed into believing that sports betting is purely "casino" behavior simply because legitimate federal markets couldn't exist before, completely missing the unimaginable possibilities that prediction markets can offer. Derivatives are useful because they allow for the transfer of risk. This is the fundamental principle by which all insurance models (and securitization) operate. Having insurance requires speculators on the other side; there is no other way in a transparent and open market without government intervention. In fact, insurance most often fails because government intervention distorts true market prices. Insurance and securitization remain among the greatest financial innovations in releasing capital efficiency. However, the problem of "events" remains: under what circumstances will an event truly become a social scourge rather than a naturally useful financial service? How can we develop an "event taxonomy"? This leads to my final point. The difference between prediction markets and other derivatives: "Prediction markets differ from other derivatives in two characteristics: 1) they are precise, and 2) they have a finite expiry date." To understand what this means, let's return to the "Market Maker 101" course. In most financial markets, the role of the Central Limit Order Book (CLOB) is to measure and provide liquidity because assets tend to have perpetual value. However, prediction markets are different: once an event catalyst is realized, liquidity collapses to zero, and there are no longer buyers or sellers on the other side. This presents a significant challenge for liquidity providers because the binary outcome of 0 or 1 invalidates the assumption of continuous dynamic hedging. More importantly, prediction markets are based on "odds," not "prices." This means that market liquidity around 2 points near the midline (50) is far higher than that around 2 points in 98% of markets, because the odds payout ratio for the latter is exponentially heavier for each point. In other words, liquidity cannot be provided continuously by spreads alone, a concept well understood by fixed-income derivatives traders (i.e., a 10 basis point fluctuation in interest rates at 4% has a completely different meaning than a 10 basis point fluctuation in interest rates at 0.5%). All of this means that in markets with extreme information asymmetry and where outcomes can be precisely predicted, professional market makers are unlikely to provide substantial liquidity. This also means that most assumptions about insiders "cashing out" through insider information likely involve very small sums. The market ultimately determines what people care about. Yes, I have top-secret information about whether Jeff Park will wear a Bitwise sweater on his next broadcast, but the chances of that market having liquidity are extremely slim. Most "anti-insider trading" arguments assume insiders will make a fortune, but this isn't true in most markets. In short, irrelevant markets don't generate natural liquidity. In fact, I bet liquidity itself precisely prices the value of that information. This is how "event taxonomy" has organically developed. So: Why is predicting the market useful to the point that its benefits outweigh its potential costs? I mentioned earlier that they are **precise**. This is one of the most virtuous aspects of prediction markets that must be emphasized. In a world where excessive financialization has led to asset prices being determined more by technical and capital flows than by fundamental analysis, prediction markets uniquely **restore the cleanest basis risk to the "truth."** In the future, if you believe you have fundamental alpha in whether Tesla's revenue will exceed expectations, you should consider betting on prediction markets rather than buying stocks—because stock prices can be distorted by other external factors. If you believe you have an advantage in non-farm payroll data, you should bet on that data, not buy or sell Eurodollar or E-mini futures. In other words, accuracy better rewards genuine excess returns, genuine research, and genuine skill. Many arguments that prediction markets prey on financial illiterate assume that "gamblers" lose money, thus making it a social vice. But in fact, prediction markets have the fairest mechanism to reward genuine skill from highly active investors. Even more powerfully, **prediction markets have no "market makers."** Unlike Las Vegas casinos that kick out high-performing investors with positive expected value (+EV), prediction markets welcome you. Citadel Securities and Charles Schwab have both announced they are exploring entry into prediction markets. Are they "preying on the economically disadvantaged"? I highly doubt it. They simply understand better than most that "the other side of speculation is insurance": your concavity (passively taking risk) is my convexity (actively hedging risk). This leads to my final note. If you've read the above, you may at least be beginning to appreciate the power of properly regulated prediction markets. If we believe the benefits outweigh the costs, we can solve both the "gambling problem" and "social ills" in various ways. However, there's a question you might realize we've skipped: "What about insider trading in markets that are crucial to the public interest? Isn't that a matter of privatization profits?" This remains a complex question, one I intend to address in another article. But I'd like to leave you with an idea, along with a book I recently read—Ashley Rindsberg's *The Gray Lady Winked*. He documents decades of not-so-accidental failures by media institutions: Durandy's suppression of Stalin's Great Famine, Castro's bizarre rise in Cuba, the promotion of Iraq's weapons of mass destruction, and the systematic whitewashing of Hitler's rise. Throughout these events, *The New York Times* (known as "The Gray Lady") has consistently been involved, using access rights, ideology, and institutional self-preservation to muddy the public's demand for the truth. If you read this book, you'll understand that it redefines "media bias" from a left/right-wing debate into a more interesting structural problem: how prestige institutions create consensus and then retroactively whitewash their mistakes. In fact, this brings us back to where we started: neither Axios nor More Perfect Union are unbiased actors in this space. It is for these reasons that you will continue to see much media criticism of prediction markets. But make no mistake: their dislike of it is precisely why you should support it. Information has a price. That's undisputed. I also often say that the opposite of misleading information is not necessarily the truth; the opposite of misleading information is actually "state-controlled information." The crux of the debate is: who has the right to set prices, who has the right to profit from them, and whether all of this happened before you even saw it. When insiders hoard asymmetric information, the incentive of money takes a backseat to the barter of power. By taxing the ignorance of others, this information can be weaponized to manipulate emotions or spread disinformation, while the truth market itself is captured. Therefore, the real argument against insider trading is less about economic efficiency and more about access. The fact is, some people trade based on what they know, while the rest of us trade based on what we are “allowed to know.” Once you see this, you won’t feel cynical about prediction markets. You’ll simply become more accurate about the world. That’s why I firmly believe that optimism about prediction markets is one of the most democratic values ​​one can hold.

data.title: Circle scenario analysis and the DeFi liquidity shock of KelpDAO
data.description:
Author: Tanay Ved, Senior Researcher at CoinMetrics; Translated by: Shaw Jinse Finance

Key Takeaways

  • Circle's $2.7 billion revenue in fiscal year 2025 comes almost entirely from USDC reserve interest. Therefore, interest rate trends, USDC supply growth, and the contract renewal with Coinbase in August 2026 are the core variables for its profit prospects.

    USDC is shifting from simple holding to more use cases: Adjusted transaction volume in 2025 increased by approximately 320% year-on-year. The Circle payment network, CCTP, and subscription services are gaining initial traction, forming a new fee revenue layer on top of reserve returns. KelpDAO's rsETH cross-chain bridge suffered an attack of approximately $290 million, triggering a widespread DeFi liquidity shock—uncollateralized rsETH was used to borrow WETH on Aave, draining liquidity and causing widespread "runs" in the affected DeFi markets. Introduction Circle (CRCL) has been listed on the NYSE for nearly a year. Its stock price surged from its IPO price of $31 on June 5th to over $280, pushing the stablecoin issuer's market capitalization to a peak of approximately $70 billion. However, the stock price subsequently retreated from its high, currently trading around $103, with a market capitalization of approximately $26 billion. As the post-IPO euphoria subsided, market attention shifted more towards the fundamentals of Circle's business model and whether it should be positioned as a reserve-earnings company or a broader payments and infrastructure platform. In this article, building upon our previous analysis of Circle's IPO and the USDC economic model, we further analyze the core drivers influencing its fundamentals. We will assess Circle's sensitivity to interest rates, the evolution of the USDC partnership with Coinbase, potential revenue limitations under the Clarity Act, and Circle's ability to expand beyond reserve earnings into other fee and subscription revenue streams. Circle's Business Model and 2025 Financial Data Circle's current business model is driven by four main factors: USDC Circulating Supply USDC Real Rate of Return on Reserve Assets Circle's share of USDC reserve earnings (after deducting distributions to partners such as Coinbase) Subscription, Service, and Other Fee Revenue In fiscal year 2025, Circle's total revenue and reserve earnings were approximately $2.7 billion, representing a 64% year-over-year increase. This growth was driven by USDC's market capitalization reaching $75.3 billion at the end of the year, maintaining a stablecoin market share of approximately 28%, while transaction volume increased by 320% year-on-year, demonstrating improved capital turnover efficiency.

    Source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro

    Reserve income linked to USDC balances and interest rates remains its core revenue source ($2.64 billion, accounting for 96%); subscription, service, and other fee revenues, although from a lower base, have begun to make a more significant contribution to total revenue ($110 million). In the fourth quarter of last year, channel distribution, transaction, and other costs totaled $461 million, primarily consisting of revenue sharing payments to partners during the distribution and use of USDC. Excluding these costs, Revenue Reduction Distribution Costs (RLDC) amounted to $1.08 billion, resulting in a full-year RLDC margin of 39%. This metric directly reflects Circle's actual revenue retained from its USDC business before deducting operating expenses.

    Source: Circle Q4 2025 Financial Report

    Therefore, the company's short-term financial performance remains primarily driven by USDC circulation, reserve earnings, and revenue sharing mechanisms (especially its partnership with Coinbase). In the following sections, we will model and analyze how changes in these drivers might affect Circle's financial condition and revenue structure.

    Circle Reserve Yield Matrix：

    Source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro

    With interest rates expected to be high (approximately 4%–4.5%), reserve yields are expected to continue to grow from 2025 levels; if the circulating supply of USDC reaches the range of $90 billion–$110 billion, reserve yields will easily exceed $4 billion.

    Source: Coinbase Q4 2025 Shareholder Letter

    In fiscal year 2025, although Circle disclosed reserve earnings of $2.63 billion, approximately $1.35 billion (51% of total reserve earnings) went to Coinbase under this revenue-sharing structure. Ultimately, Circle's revenue after deducting distribution costs (RLDC) was $1.08 billion, with a profit margin of 39%.

data.title: Coinbase and Gemini Face New York Lawsuit Over Unlicensed Prediction Markets and Gambling Claims
data.description:
A growing dispute over prediction markets has reached New York courts, with state regulators accusing two major crypto-linked platforms of running unlicensed gambling services under the guise of financial trading.

New York Moves Against Crypto Prediction Platforms Over Gambling Claims

New York Attorney General Letitia James has launched legal action against Coinbase Financial Markets and Gemini Titan, alleging both firms are operating illegal gambling schemes through their prediction market products.

The complaint argues that users are effectively betting on real-world outcomes such as sports results, entertainment events and elections without the required state licences.

.@Gemini and @coinbase's so-called prediction markets are just illegal gambling operations that expose young people to addictive platforms.

Gambling by another name is still gambling. I'm suing to stop these platforms from breaking the law.https://t.co/DosDKe2un1

— NY AG James (@NewYorkStateAG) April 21, 2026

James says the platforms should have obtained approval from the New York State Gaming Commission before offering these services.

Instead, they allegedly allowed trading activity that mirrors casino-style wagering while avoiding regulatory oversight.

Are Prediction Markets Just Another Form Of Gambling

At the centre of the case is the question of whether prediction contracts are financial instruments or simply bets in disguise.

The state argues that users have no control over outcomes, making the activity equivalent to gambling under New York law.

James said,

“Gambling by another name is still gambling, and it is not exempt from regulation under our state laws and Constitution.”

The complaint also highlights age restrictions.

While New York requires participants in mobile sports betting to be at least 21, both platforms reportedly allowed users aged 18 and above to take part.

Heavy Penalties Sought Including Profit Forfeiture And Fines

The Attorney General is seeking wide-ranging penalties, including the return of profits made through the alleged unlawful activity, civil fines equal to three times those profits, and compensation for affected customers.

Update: @NewYorkStateAG has also filed a motion for temporary restraining order and preliminary injunction against both Gemini and Coinbase, seeking permanent injunctive relief, an accounting, restitution, disgorgement, damages, civil penalties, and costs. https://t.co/OF7CGnrYLepic.twitter.com/7TuYkLHI3R

— Daniel Wallach (@WALLACHLEGAL) April 21, 2026

The state is also pushing for restrictions that would prevent the companies from accepting wagers from anyone under 21 and from advertising on college campuses.

Officials argue the platforms also bypassed tax obligations that apply to licensed gambling operators, reducing funds that would otherwise support public education, youth programmes and gambling addiction services.

Coinbase Pushes Back As Gemini Stays Silent

Coinbase has rejected the claims and vowed to continue defending its position.

Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal said the company intends to pursue federal oversight of prediction markets.

“Coinbase will continue to fight for the federal oversight of these markets that Congress intended.”

About the NY lawsuit news - prediction markets are federally regulated national exchanges, registered with the CFTC. This issue is proceeding in New York federal court as we speak. Coinbase will continue to fight for the federal oversight of these markets that Congress intended.

— Paul Grewal (@iampaulgrewal) April 21, 2026

Gemini, founded by Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, did not immediately respond to the lawsuit.

Both firms only entered the prediction market space in mid-December, rolling out services that are now available across all 50 US states.

State Vs Federal Power Struggle Over Prediction Markets

The lawsuit is unfolding amid a broader jurisdictional battle between state regulators and federal authorities.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has argued it holds exclusive authority over prediction markets, warning that state-level rules create conflicting obligations for companies operating nationally.

The CFTC recently sued several states, including Arizona, Connecticut and Illinois, in an effort to block local attempts to regulate the sector.

Its chair, Michael Selig, warned that inconsistent oversight could harm consumers rather than protect them.

Courts And Regulators Split As Industry Gains Momentum

Court rulings have so far been mixed.

A federal appeals court in Philadelphia recently sided with prediction market operator Kalshi, ruling that New Jersey regulators could not block its contracts.

However, Nevada has secured temporary injunctions preventing both Kalshi and Coinbase from operating within the state.

ICYMI: Nevada state court judge issues preliminary injunction barring Coinbase from offering sports-, election-, and entertainment-related event contracts, holds that CEA does not preempt Nevada's gambling laws. Next up: The PI hearing vs. Kalshi on 4/3.https://t.co/TJXxG7FFLYpic.twitter.com/0vpQcMOgMS

— Daniel Wallach (@WALLACHLEGAL) March 28, 2026

Kalshi has also taken legal action against New York regulators in a separate federal case, while its dispute remains unresolved.

Lawmakers Warn Of Insider Trading Risks And Political Influence

On Capitol Hill, at least eight bills have been introduced since January targeting prediction markets, with concerns focusing on insider trading and market manipulation.

NEW -- RITCHIE TORRES (D-N.Y.) will introduce a bill on this.

Bill will be called the Public Integrity in Financial Prediction Markets Act of 2026

Description, per a source:

This bill prohibits federal elected officials, political appointees, and Executive Branch employees… https://t.co/eZZ9BmAMgJ

— Jake Sherman (@JakeSherman) January 3, 2026

Attention intensified after reports that a user made around $400,000 from a prediction tied to political developments in Venezuela, alongside trading activity linked to US military strikes on Iran.

Senator Jeff Merkley described the sector as “a real danger to our democracy and ripe for exploitation by public officials with insider information,” while Senator Todd Young urged Congress to act despite political sensitivities.

Some lawmakers have also pointed to links between industry platforms and political figures.

Donald Trump Jr. is an adviser to both Polymarket and Kalshi, and a Trump-linked media group has announced plans for its own prediction platform, Truth Predict.

Five specific instances have been examined where massive bets were placed on market moving events just before the news was made public.

Trump Jr. is an investor and advisor for #Polymarket and #Kalshi, the prediction platforms where many of these trades occurred.

— IMMRBRØWN (@IMMRBR0WN) April 21, 2026

The White House has denied any conflict of interest, saying insider trading rules already apply and must be enforced across all markets.

data.title: Hermes Agent vs. Rotifer Agent: Intelligent Evolutionary Path Selection
data.description:
Author: Zhang Feng

In the spring of 2026, the AI ​​Agent field witnessed a remarkable paradigm shift. Hermes Agent garnered a staggering 66,000 stars on GitHub, topping the global programming application charts, while Rotifer Protocol sparked heated debate in the developer community with its biological metaphor of a "genetic paradigm." Both claim to enable AI to "self-evolve," but upon closer examination, they represent two drastically different evolutionary philosophies—Hermes follows a "personal growth" path, while Rotifer follows an "ecological inheritance" path. This divergence is not merely a difference in technical architecture, but also reflects a fundamental choice of path for the evolution of intelligent agents.

data.title: Kalshi and Polymarket to offer perpetual futures; Trump extends ceasefire
data.description:
Headline

▌Kalshi and Polymarket to Offer Perpetual Futures Trading

On April 22, following competitor Kalshi's plan to offer cryptocurrency perpetual futures trading, Polymarket also began expanding its perpetual futures trading business.

▌Trump Announces Extension of Ceasefire Against Iran

On April 22, US President Trump posted on "Real Social Media" on the afternoon of April 21 local time, stating that at the request of the Pakistani Army Chief of Staff and Prime Minister, the US would suspend military strikes against Iran, extend the ceasefire period, and require Iran to first submit a unified negotiation proposal. During this period, the US military will continue to maintain a naval blockade against Iran while maintaining a state of military readiness. He stated that the ceasefire will continue until Iran submits a proposal and completes negotiations, "regardless of the outcome."

Policy

▌Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh supports crypto inclusion in the financial system; lawmakers raise regulatory questions

On April 22, Kevin Warsh stated at a Senate hearing that digital assets "have become part of the U.S. financial system" and support their inclusion to provide investors with more opportunities and protection. This statement is seen as a policy signal of a generally open approach to the crypto industry should he become the Federal Reserve Chair. Warsh has previously stated that Bitcoin is "an important asset that helps policymakers." However, Elizabeth Warren expressed concerns during the hearing, mentioning the potential risks of "sock puppets" in the crypto space and emphasizing the need for stronger regulation and prevention of abuse.

▌SEC Chairman: Moving Forward to Establish a Regulatory Framework for Digital Assets and Proposing the "A-C-T" Strategy

In his keynote speech at the Economic Club of Washington, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins pointed out that in his first year in office, he has pushed the SEC back to its core mission of "protecting investors, maintaining market order, and promoting capital formation," and criticized past regulations for being overly expansionary, increasing market friction and uncertainty. Paul Atkins added that the SEC is accelerating the establishment of a clear regulatory foundation for digital assets, collaborating with other regulatory agencies and Congress, and has proposed the "A-C-T" strategy, which includes advancing regulatory modernization, clarifying regulatory boundaries, and transforming the rule system based on fundamental principles, to enhance the competitiveness of the U.S. market and consolidate its position as a global investment and business environment.

...

▌New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase and Gemini, Alleging Prediction Markets Constitute "Illegal Gambling"

On April 21, Letitia James filed a lawsuit against Coinbase and Gemini, accusing them of providing "disguised gambling" services through their prediction market platforms, violating New York state law. Regulators argue that such transactions based on event outcomes (such as sports or elections) are essentially gambling activities, particularly questioning the allowance for users aged 18 to 21, while New York law stipulates a minimum age of 21 for sports betting. Prosecutors are seeking substantial fines and forfeiture of proceeds, including at least $2.2 billion from Coinbase and at least $1.2 billion from Gemini. Neither company has officially responded, but Coinbase stated that prediction markets are regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, implying that their legality falls under federal jurisdiction.

▌Philippine Securities and Exchange Commission Issues Investment Warning for 7 Crypto Trading Platforms

On April 21, the Philippine Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) issued an investor warning, advising the public against investing in seven cryptocurrency trading platforms: dYdX, Aevo, gTrade, Pacifica, Orderly, Deriv, and Ostium. The SEC stated that these platforms are not registered with the Commission and have not obtained the necessary authorizations required by the Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) framework. The SEC also warned that individuals promoting these platforms in the Philippines could face criminal charges, including a fine of up to 5,000,000 Philippine pesos (approximately US$89,000) or imprisonment for up to 21 years.

...

▌Iran Agrees to Suspension of Military Fighting, But the War Is Not Over

On April 22, local time, in response to Trump's statement regarding extending the ceasefire, Iranian state television stated that Iran has become the victor on the battlefield. Control of the Strait of Hormuz is an extremely valuable bargaining chip that Iran has gained in this war. Iran has agreed to a suspension of military fighting, but the war is not over. At the same time, Iranian state television also pointed out that Iran must be wary of any statements suggesting that it must participate in negotiations or its enemies will attack it. Even if the naval blockade is lifted, Iran's participation in negotiations must be conditional on not raising any issues that infringe upon Iran's independence and dignity, with the primary focus being Iran's defense and missile capabilities, as well as its nuclear capabilities and technology.

... (CCTV) On April 22, the Iranian negotiating team announced to the US through Pakistani intermediaries that, for various reasons, they would not travel to Islamabad, Pakistan on the 22nd and currently have no possibility of participating in the negotiations. Sources indicated that after Pakistan intervened to mediate and requested a ceasefire from the US, Iran accepted the ceasefire agreement and agreed to conduct subsequent negotiations to end the war based on a ten-point framework it proposed, which had already been accepted by the US. Pakistan also explicitly stated that the US accepted this framework. However, in the following days, the US began to renege on its promises. Furthermore, in the first round of negotiations held in Islamabad, the US made many excessive demands, effectively violating the initial framework, leading to a stalemate in the round of talks. Sources also stated that in the exchanges over the past few days, the US insisted on its excessive demands, which violated the absolute rights of the Iranian people, and the exchanges between the two sides did not achieve any substantial progress. Therefore, Iran finally announced today that, under these circumstances, participating in the negotiations would be a waste of time, as the US is preventing any substantive agreement from being reached. (CCTV) Blockchain Applications ▌Digital Bank Revolut's IPO Valuation Could Reach $200 Billion The Financial Times, citing anonymous investor sources, reported that UK digital bank Revolut plans to seek a valuation of $150 billion to $200 billion in its future IPO, a significant increase from its previous valuation of $75 billion. CEO ONik Storonsky also revealed that Revolut is preparing for a new round of secondary share sales in the second half of 2026, with a valuation potentially exceeding $100 billion. ▌Bezos' AI Lab Reportedly Close to $38 Billion Valuation The Financial Times, citing sources familiar with the matter, reported that Project Prometheus, the AI ​​lab run by Jeff Bezos, is nearing the completion of a new round of financing. The round of funding is estimated at approximately $10 billion, with a post-money valuation of around $38 billion. JPMorgan Chase and BlackRock participated in this round. The deal has not yet been finalized. Project Prometheus is not building general-purpose chatbots. It focuses on AI that can understand the physical world. The goal is to integrate its models into engineering, design, and manufacturing processes. Last month, Axios, citing sources familiar with the matter, stated that the company prefers to focus on prototyping, materials, and early-stage engineering rather than directly replacing factory workers with robots. Cryptocurrency

On April 22, according to on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, Kelp DAO attackers have transferred over 31,000 ETH to tools such as mixers and privacy protocols for laundering, amounting to approximately $72.58 million. In the past 15 hours, two storage addresses have begun making multiple small transactions. Currently, the on-chain addresses retain 75,125 ETH, worth $174 million, and these transactions are still ongoing.

Cryptocurrency

▌KelpDAO attackers have transferred over 31,000 ETH to tools such as mixers and privacy protocols for laundering, amounting to approximately $72.58 million.

▌Tether issues 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network

On April 21, according to Onchainlens monitoring, Tether issued 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. Furthermore, in the past two days, Tether has cumulatively issued 2 billion USDT on the Ethereum network.

▌Coinbase to launch Diem (DIEM) spot trading

On April 22, Coinbase will launch Diem (DIEM) spot trading. If liquidity conditions are met and the trading is available in supported regions, the DIEM-USD trading pair will be launched.

▌Coinbase to Launch Sign (SIGN) Spot Trading

On April 21st, Coinbase will launch spot trading for Sign (SIGN). The SIGN-USD trading pair will open later when liquidity conditions are met, applicable to regions that support the relevant trading.

▌Coinbase to Launch Opengradient (OPG)

On April 21st, according to official news, Coinbase will launch spot trading for Opengradient (OPG).

**Important Economic Developments** **Trump: I Will Be Disappointed If the New Fed Chair Doesn't Cut Rates** **April 21, US President Trump: I will be disappointed if the new Fed chair doesn't cut rates.** **Wash: Agreed to Sell Assets According to Agreement with the Office of Ethics** Fed Chair nominee Warsh: (When asked about his investments) He has agreed to sell assets according to an agreement with the Office of Ethics. **Wash: Independence Depends on the Fed** Fed Chair nominee Warsh: I will be independent of Trump's opinions. Trump tends to demand that the FOMC cut rates. Independence depends on the Fed.

▌Iran Did Not Request an Extension of the Ceasefire; Officials Say US Move Was to Buy Time for a Surprise Attack

On April 22, local time, Iran announced that it had not requested an extension of the ceasefire. Regarding US President Trump's earlier statement on extending the ceasefire, an advisor to the Speaker of the Iranian Islamic Parliament stated on social media, "Trump's proposal to extend the ceasefire is meaningless. The loser has no right to set conditions. Continued blockade and bombing are tantamount to a military response. Moreover, Trump's proposal to extend the ceasefire is clearly to buy time for a surprise attack. Now is the time for Iran to seize the initiative." (CCTV)

▌White House: Vance's Trip to Pakistan Cancelled

On April 22, a White House official said on the 21st that US Vice President Vance's planned trip to Pakistan that day had been cancelled.

According to CNN, the official said in a statement that given President Trump's post on social media "confirming that the United States is awaiting a proposal from Iran, the visit to Pakistan will not take place today." Updates on the face-to-face talks between the US and Iran will be released by the White House. (Xinhua)

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 100%

On April 22, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Fed raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 0%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 100%. The probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 1.7%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 98.3%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin still be called "digital gold" under the geopolitical crisis? Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been substantiated by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: DeFi is caught in the most dangerous prisoner's dilemma in history.
data.description:
More than 40 hours after the theft, the chain reaction triggered by Kelp DAO continues to unfold, involving not only more and more well-known projects such as Aave, LayerZero, and Arbitrum, but even reaching the point where some popular narratives are facing a death sentence.

Well-known KOL Feng Wuxiang stated on the X platform that only ETH is safe now, and ARB has authorized the freezing and transfer of customer assets. There shouldn't be any true L2 anymore. L2 rose with Arbitrum, and it will also die with Arbitrum.

Another well-known KOL, Lanhu, stated that the biggest loser in this Kelp incident was not Aave, nor Kelp, but LayerZero. However, they were too short-sighted and failed to see the true nature of the event. The essence of this event is not that it disproves L2 (let's leave aside fake L2), but that it disproves cross-chain bridges.

More and more heated opinions are emerging in the public sphere, with the parties involved sticking to their own stories and arguing amongst themselves. This makes the Kelp DAO theft a typical window into the division of responsibility for security incidents and the conflict between pragmatism and technological fundamentalism. I. L0 Falsified? Cross-Chain Bridges Become the Biggest Losers The key point of the incident is LayerZero's detailed report on the hacker attack released yesterday, which initially identifies the attackers as the North Korean-backed Lazarus Group. The attack was achieved by poisoning the downstream RPC infrastructure on which its decentralized verifier network (DVN) relies. The attackers controlled some RPC nodes and, in conjunction with a DDoS attack, induced the system to switch to malicious nodes, thereby forging cross-chain transactions. "Using compromised nodes to poison RPC infrastructure, combined with DDoS attacks on unaffected RPCs to force failover, is a highly complex tactic. It's essentially an infrastructure war," commented Samuel Tse, Head of Investments and Partnerships at Animoca Brands. At the end of the report, LayerZero stated that the protocol functioned exactly as expected throughout the incident. No vulnerabilities were found in the protocol. A core feature of the LayerZero architecture is modular security, and in this case, it perfectly achieved its intended goal, isolating the entire attack within a single application—zero risk of infection across the entire system, and other OFT or OApps remained unaffected. This complete abdication of responsibility became the trigger for a huge public backlash, with many prominent industry figures expressing dissatisfaction with LayerZero's performance in this incident. "L0 completely absolves itself of responsibility, blaming everything on KelpDAO's configuration errors in the entire article, while claiming no fault of its own. Unbelievable. Why was a 1/1 configuration allowed? Why was the internal RPC list obtained by the attacker? Why did the failover logic directly trust the poisoned RPC after the DDoS attack, instead of immediately stopping verification or doing anything at all?" Renowned industry researcher CM questioned rhetorically. "This deliberate avoidance makes me very uncomfortable. The statement clearly states that 'the protocol operates exactly as expected.' The attack is described as RPC nodes being compromised and RPC poisoning. But RPC poisoning is not like that; their own infrastructure was compromised and damaged. Given that the statement doesn't explain how the intrusion occurred, I won't rush to re-enable bridging," said renowned DeFi developer banteg. Kelp DAO also issued a statement, clarifying that the single validator (1/1) configuration that led to the attack was not a choice made ignoring advice, but rather the default setting in the official LayerZero guidelines. Furthermore, the validator network (DVN) exploited by the attackers is LayerZero's own infrastructure. According to Dune's analysis, 47% of the 2,665 OApp contracts based on LayerZero use a 1/1 DVN configuration, i.e., a single-validation mechanism, which dramatically amplifies the industry's risks. More alarming than the occurrence of the problem itself is the party's refusal to acknowledge and evade their error. As the leading player in cross-chain communication and the Layer 0 narrative, LayerZero has hundreds of crypto projects using its cross-chain infrastructure to bridge tokens and assets across different chains. If it continues with this arrogant attitude, it will inevitably further erode the industry's confidence in it. Public opinion generally believes that although LayerZero was not directly hacked, its reputation suffered the most damage—it must pay the price for "allowing weak configurations," otherwise the cross-chain narrative will collapse. In other words, LayerZero not only needs to propose clear technical improvements, but also needs to take more responsibility for its asset compensation plan. II. Is Layer2 Dead? Arbitrum's Unconventional Freeze Discussions about Layer2 stem from Arbitrum's freeze. This afternoon, the Arbitrum Security Committee announced that it had taken emergency action to rescue 30,766 ETH, currently worth $71 million, stored by the hackers in the Arbitrum One address. Arbitrum also stated that, after extensive technical investigation and deliberation, the Security Committee identified and implemented a technical solution to transfer funds to a secure location without affecting the state of any other chain or Arbitrum users. The original address holding the funds is no longer able to access them, and only the Arbitrum administration can take further action to transfer these funds, an action that will be coordinated with relevant parties. According to industry interpretations, the Arbitrum Security Committee used a privileged state overriding transaction type (part of ArbOS, but essentially never used) that allows an attacker's private key to still sign transactions, but the ETH at that address is transferred by the chain itself. This special transaction type completely bypasses the attacker's private key and can only be injected by the chain itself (via the sequencer/ArbOS upgrade path, controlled by the Arbitrum Security Committee). It is understood that the Arbitrum Security Committee consists of 12 individuals elected by the Arbitrum DAO, and any decision requires the approval of 9/12 of them. This has caused a great stir. Previously, it was widely believed that Arbitrum, as a representative Layer 2 blockchain, lacked the ability and authority to handle users' ETH assets, as this violated the decentralized spirit of blockchain. In past hacking incidents, stolen USDT and USDC were often frozen immediately by Tether and Circle to minimize user losses. ETH, as a native blockchain asset, has never been frozen and transferred by the blockchain itself, exceeding the expectations of most users. Many viewpoints support Arbitrum's approach, arguing that "all companies, banks, and formal financial institutions will eventually adopt a two-tier architecture. Operating like a centralized entity in critical moments is not a flaw, but an advantage." However, this is not the case for many tech enthusiasts. "No private keys, no authorization, direct transfers." Many believe Arbitrum's move redefines the decentralization of Layer 2, making them feel insecure on Layer 2. Blue Fox bluntly states that this incident directly crosses the core ideological red line of DeFi: "Not Your keys, not your coins." This incident returns to the classic crypto dilemma: pragmatic security vs. fully decentralized security. Conclusion When LayerZero claimed that "the protocol is operating exactly as expected," it preserved technical correctness but lost public opinion and trust. When Arbitrum used privileged transactions to transfer $71 million worth of ETH, it saved user funds but severely damaged the decentralized narrative of Layer2. The Kelp hack brought two of the most popular narratives to the forefront: Are cross-chain bridges infrastructure or risk amplifiers? Is Layer2 a reliable extension of Ethereum or a secondary bank disguised as decentralization? Due to the breach of LayerZero's single validator mechanism, Arbitrum used a centralized special voting mechanism to mitigate the losses for LayerZero and the Kelp DAO. This creates a highly ironic closed loop: a self-proclaimed decentralized protocol collapses due to its "single point of vulnerability," only to ultimately have to rely on the "centralized privileges" of another protocol to survive. This forces the entire industry to confront a question that has never been answered directly: when the ideal of decentralization collides with the security costs of reality, which side are we willing to sacrifice? The grand narrative of the discussion is one focus of public opinion, while the user compensation plan is another, more practical focus. Even though Arbitrum recovered over $70 million through technical means, Aave still has nearly $200 million in bad debts. How can users' interests be properly protected and safeguarded? In most hacking incidents, losses of tens of millions of dollars are devastating for a protocol, and user claims usually end in failure. However, this incident involves leading projects like Aave and Layerzero, making their bad debt handling plans highly anticipated. Aave today proposed two possible bad debt handling solutions. The first is a socialized sharing of losses among all rsETH holders (chain-wide sharing), with Kelp DAO uniformly reducing the value of all rsETH (mainnet + L2) (approximately 15% decoupling). The second is to only have L2-based rsETH holders bear all losses, while mainnet rsETH maintains its original value. However, neither Kelp DAO nor LayerZero has discussed their role in the compensation plan. LayerZero's attempt to absolve itself of responsibility in its report clearly indicates that the project believes there is no obligation to compensate without liability. However, for a protocol with a valuation of billions of dollars, considered the underlying foundation by hundreds of projects, to choose "technical exemption" in the face of massive losses caused by DVN's default configuration is a huge irony of the definition of "underlying infrastructure." This is a classic prisoner's dilemma, where all parties in the crisis are trying to minimize their own losses through "profit-sharing" rather than repairing the industry's trust deficit by sharing responsibility. Judging from the negative impact of this event on all parties in the industry, this will be the most dangerous prisoner's dilemma in the history of DeFi.

data.title: Golden Web3.0 Daily Report | Crypto Exchange Grinex Ceases Operations
data.description:
DeFi data

1. Total market value of DeFi tokens: US$53.848 billion

DeFi Total Market Cap Data Source: coingecko

2. Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours: $48.41

Trading volume of decentralized exchanges in the past 24 hours. Data source: coingecko

3. Assets locked in DeFi: $857.34 billion$100 million

Top 10 DeFi Projects by Locked Assets and Total Value Locked. Data Source: defillama

NFT Data

1. Total Market Value of NFTs: $7.896 Billion

2.24 hours NFT transaction volume 1.595 billion USD

Headline

Russian Crypto Exchange Grinex Ceases Operations

On April 21, Grinex, a major Russian cryptocurrency exchange, ceased operations last Wednesday due to a suspected major cyberattack, reportedly incurring losses exceeding 1 billion rubles (approximately $13 million). The platform claimed on Telegram that the attack showed “indications of foreign intelligence agency involvement.” Grinex is the successor to the previously sanctioned and shut-down Garantex exchange, providing the Russian economy with a financial channel to circumvent sanctions. In 2025, it processed nearly $100 billion in trading volume for the sanctioned stablecoin A7A5. Experts point out that Grinex's shutdown is not only due to the hacking attack itself, but more importantly, it removed the trading platform that Russian companies rely on to convert rubles into usable international currencies, causing "serious damage" to Russia's infrastructure for circumventing sanctions and making it more difficult for Russia to escape the pressure of sanctions on the Russian economy. The trading platform was already sanctioned by Western authorities such as the US, UK, and EU in August 2025. Meanwhile, the Russian economy continues to weaken; Putin revealed that GDP fell by 1.8% in January and February this year, and seaborne oil exports may fall to their lowest level since 2023.

    AI Hot Topics

    1. OpenAI Launches Codex Labs

    April 21st news: OpenAI stated that Codex's weekly active users jumped from 3 million to 4 million in two weeks.

    2. Trump: Anthropic is "doing well," willing to consider resuming cooperation

    April 21st - US President Trump stated in an interview with CNBC on Tuesday that his administration believes Anthropic is "doing well" and he is willing to consider reaching an agreement that would allow the company to resume contractual cooperation with Department of Defense officials. Trump instructed federal agencies in February to remove Anthropic from procurement contracts. Anthropic's CEO met with White House officials last week in an attempt to repair relations. The White House described the meeting as productive and constructive.

    ... Trump said on Tuesday, "They came to the White House a few days ago, and we had very in-depth conversations with them. I think they are maturing. They are very smart, and I think they can play an important role. I like smart people." On April 21, according to official news, the 0G Foundation and Alibaba Cloud have reached a cooperation agreement to provide AI agents with direct access to Alibaba's 1000 Questions and Answers series of large language models through blockchain infrastructure. 0G stated that it obtains access credentials for 1000 Questions and Answers through API and embeds them into its own infrastructure, enabling AI agents to directly call the 1000 Questions and Answers models based on a tokenization mechanism, reducing traditional account management, fiat currency billing, and manual configuration processes. This cooperation aims to promote AI from API access to programmable, tokenized infrastructure, supporting AI agents in natural language generation, information processing, decision-making, and multi-step workflow execution.

    4. Justin Sun Makes Online Appearance at HTX Genesis Hackathon: AI Agent is the Core Driving Force for Web3's Advancement to Intelligence

    Justin Sun, Global Advisor of Huobi HTX, made a significant online appearance at the opening ceremony of the HTX Genesis Hackathon in Hong Kong, sharing his latest assessment of the application prospects of AI Agent technology in the Web3 field and the future development direction of the industry. He pointed out that AI Agent will replace traditional interaction methods and become the core driving force of the next stage of the Web3 ecosystem. He explained that past Web3 applications often involved extremely high learning barriers and complex on-chain interactions, while the introduction of AI Agent will drive decentralized applications (DApps) to evolve from "manual operation" to "intention-driven" based on natural language. This means that AI Agent can autonomously understand user needs, automatically plan paths, and complete complex on-chain settlements such as cross-chain transactions.

    ... This paradigm shift towards intelligence and autonomy will not only greatly unleash on-chain productivity but will also become a new breakthrough point for breaking through the bottleneck of Web3 user growth and leading the industry towards true mass adoption. It is reported that the HTX Genesis Hackathon, hosted by HTX DAO and B.AI, and co-hosted by TinTinLand and OpenCity, attracted numerous developer communities, investment institutions, project teams, trading platforms, and public chain ecosystem participants. The 2026 Forbes AI 50 list was released, with OpenAI and Anthropic leading the way. The 2026 Forbes AI 50 list was released, with industry leaders such as OpenAI and Anthropic remaining giants on the list. They have received unprecedented funding from top Silicon Valley venture capital firms and tech giants and are reportedly preparing for a major IPO. These two AI giants have raised a total of $242.6 billion, accounting for approximately 80% of the $305.6 billion raised by the companies on this year's AI 50 list. The widespread adoption of their products has driven strong revenue growth: as of the end of February, OpenAI's annualized revenue reportedly exceeded $25 billion; in early April, Anthropic announced its revenue run rate had surpassed $30 billion. These two AI labs, with products like Claude Code and OpenAI Codex, have become dominant players in markets such as programming, forcing companies like Cursor, valued at $29.3 billion, to innovate for survival.

    DeFi Hot Topics

    1. KelpDAO Attackers Transfer 50,700 ETH Again

    On April 21, according to AiYi's monitoring, KelpDAO attackers just transferred another 50,700 ETH to two new addresses, equivalent to approximately $118 million at current prices.

    The receiving address has not yet been transferred or sold. 2. Aave TVL drops to $16.432 billion, with some funds flowing into Spark. On April 21, according to Lookonchain monitoring, funds continued to flow out of Aave, with some flowing into Spark. Data shows that Aave TVL has dropped to $16.432 billion, a decrease of approximately $9.94 billion from the previous level; meanwhile, Spark TVL rose to $4.552 billion, an increase of approximately $825 million. 3. Tether issues 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. On April 21, according to Onchainlens monitoring, Tether issued 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum network. In addition, Tether has issued a total of 2 billion USDT on the Ethereum network in the past two days.

    4. Aave: WETH Reserves in the Ethereum Core V3 Markets Unfrozen

    On April 21, Aave officially updated the latest developments regarding the rsETH hack: WETH reserves in the Ethereum Core V3 market have been unfrozen, and users can now supply WETH to Ethereum Core V3 again. The LTV (Loan-to-Value Ratio) of WETH remains at 0. WETH reserves on Ethereum Prime, Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, and Linea remain frozen. Aave service providers will continue to pursue follow-up steps and will provide updates in a timely manner.

    ...

    5.Kelp DAO attackers transfer 30,800 ETH to a special address

    April 21st news: According to PeckShield monitoring, Kelp DAO attackers transferred 30,800 ETH to a special address starting with 0x00000, possibly for ETH burning.

    Disclaimer: Jinse Finance, as a blockchain information platform, publishes articles for informational purposes only and does not constitute actual investment advice. Please establish correct investment concepts and be sure to enhance your risk awareness.

data.title: The revolutionary fusion of AI Tokens, blockchain Tokens, and fully homomorphic encryption
data.description:
On the afternoon of April 21st, Xiao Feng, Chairman of Wanxiang Blockchain and Chairman and CEO of HashKey Group, delivered a keynote speech entitled "Innovation of Intelligent Agent Economic Model - The Fusion Revolution of AI Token, Blockchain Token and Fully Homomorphic Encryption" at the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, and released the 2026 "Token Economics White Paper". The following is a transcript of the event, with slight modifications that do not affect the original meaning.

Xiao Feng: Good afternoon, everyone!

After yesterday's information "bombardment" and this morning, I believe everyone is quite tired. This afternoon's forum is about RWA. First, we will release the 2026 "Token Economics White Paper".

To recap, HashKey began releasing this series of white papers in 2023.

In the 2024 version, a "three-token model" was specifically proposed, namely equity tokens, utility tokens, and non-fungible tokens such as NFTs. Over the past decade, we've found that a single token might be sufficient for the most basic protocols; however, when targeting applications, B2B and B2C users, a single token layer, such as utility tokens, may not be enough to establish a sound, comprehensive, and effective economic incentive mechanism. Utility tokens are an incentive mechanism for the community, while equity tokens are an incentive mechanism for the entrepreneurial team and shareholders—this was version 1.0 of our "Token Economics White Paper." Now, we've entered version 3.0. This time, we focus on how the intelligent agent economy brought about by AI Agents can be combined with Crypto and blockchain. Therefore, the title of my opening speech today is: Innovation in the Intelligent Agent Economic Model—What Innovation Will the Combination of AI Tokens, Blockchain Tokens, and Privacy Computing Technologies such as ZK and Fully Homomorphic Encryption Bring to the Intelligent Agent Economy? From a business innovation perspective, the main characteristics of blockchain technology can be summarized in two points: First, the blockchain network is a trustless network.
